title: Interpret Map Overview Location Types And System Areas
summary: Use the map overview to identify major system areas and distinguish lane versus station location types as presented in the training source.
when_to_use: Use this reference when viewing the RMS map overview to orient yourself to the overall system layout, separate major areas such as sorter side, charger side, and tipper side, and identify whether displayed map elements are lanes or stations.
not_for: Do not use this source alone to assign operational meaning to a special location when the source states that local site usage determines whether it functions as a sort location, tipper location, or other use.
Do not use this reference to assume RMS itself distinguishes the operational meaning of special locations.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the map overview, identify major system areas, and describe displayed location types using the source terms lane, station, and special location.

safety_notes: This source segment is a visual interpretation reference and does not describe physical intervention.
Do not assume the operational meaning of a special location from RMS alone when the source says the distinction depends on local usage.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the map overview screen
Visual access to the system map display

access_required: Access to the RMS map overview screen
system_preconditions: The map overview screen is available to open or click.

visual_references.description: Training frame showing the map overview used to distinguish major system areas and the visual difference between lanes and stations.

steps.title: Open the map overview
steps.purpose: Access the system-level map view used in the training explanation.
steps.instruction: Open or click the map overview screen that gives an overview of the system.
steps.healthy_condition: A system-level overview map is visible.
steps.failure_condition: The overview map is not available or does not display.

actions.label: Open map overview
actions.exact_action: Open or click the map overview screen that gives an overview of the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overall system map view referenced as the overview screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the map overview screen cannot be opened or viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Clicking this view gives an overview of the system; the presenter references separating sorter side, charger side, and tipper side from this map.

steps.title: Identify major system areas
steps.purpose: Orient the viewer to the major areas shown on the system map.
steps.instruction: Look across the map to identify the major system areas referenced in the source, including sorter side, charger side, and tipper side.
steps.expected_result: The displayed map is mentally separated into the major areas named in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: Sorter side, charger side, and tipper side can be visually distinguished on the overview.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed areas cannot be matched to the source-described major system areas.

actions.label: Review system areas
actions.exact_action: Look across the map to identify the major system areas referenced in the source, including sorter side, charger side, and tipper side.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overall layout used to separate sorter side, charger side, and tipper side.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed map does not allow the major areas to be matched to sorter side, charger side, and tipper side.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Clicking this view gives an overview of the system; the presenter references separating sorter side, charger side, and tipper side from this map.

steps.title: Identify lanes on the map
steps.purpose: Recognize the lane visual type used on the overview.
steps.instruction: Identify the light blue or grayish square blocks on the map as lanes.
steps.expected_result: Light blue or grayish square blocks are recognized as lanes.
steps.healthy_condition: The viewer can point to the light blue or grayish square blocks and label them as lanes.
steps.failure_condition: The viewer cannot match the lane visual type to the source description.

actions.label: Recognize lane visual type
actions.exact_action: Identify the light blue or grayish square blocks on the map as lanes.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The light blue or grayish square blocks identified in the training as lanes.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed map element cannot be matched to the source-described lane visual type.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There are two variants of location: a lane, shown as light blue grayish square blocks, and stations, shown as orange ones.

steps.title: Identify stations on the map
steps.purpose: Recognize the station visual type used on the overview.
steps.instruction: Identify the orange map elements as stations.
steps.expected_result: Orange map elements are recognized as stations.
steps.healthy_condition: The viewer can point to the orange elements and label them as stations.
steps.failure_condition: The viewer cannot match the station visual type to the source description.

actions.label: Recognize station visual type
actions.exact_action: Identify the orange map elements as stations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The orange map elements identified in the training as stations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed map element cannot be matched to the source-described station visual type.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There are two variants of location: a lane, shown as light blue grayish square blocks, and stations, shown as orange ones.

steps.title: Interpret special locations carefully
steps.purpose: Prevent incorrect assumptions about operational meaning when RMS uses generic station concepts.
steps.instruction: When reviewing a location on the map, note that RMS uses a generic station concept and that a special location may represent different operational uses depending on site usage, such as a sort location or a tipper location.
steps.expected_result: The viewer understands that RMS naming alone may not define the operational function of a special location.
steps.healthy_condition: Special locations are treated as requiring local interpretation rather than assumed meaning from RMS alone.
steps.failure_condition: A special location is assumed to have a fixed operational meaning based only on the RMS display.

actions.label: Review special location meaning
actions.exact_action: When reviewing a location on the map, note that RMS uses a generic station concept and that a special location may represent different operational uses depending on site usage, such as a sort location or a tipper location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The map overview context where the presenter explains that RMS treats stations generically and special locations may have site-specific meaning.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if a special location's operational meaning cannot be confirmed locally.
Do not assume the operational meaning of a special location from RMS alone when the source says the distinction depends on local usage.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The presenter says that in RMS world, a station is a station.
A special location could be a sort location and can also be a tipper location; the system does not know the difference by itself.

steps.title: Communicate the observed location using source terms
steps.purpose: Ensure map observations are described using the terminology supported by the source.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the observed area and location type using the source terms lane, station, and special location rather than assuming the system itself distinguishes the operational meaning.
steps.expected_result: The observed map area and location type are communicated using source-supported terminology.
steps.healthy_condition: Descriptions use lane, station, and special location correctly and avoid unsupported assumptions.
steps.failure_condition: Descriptions assign unsupported operational meaning or use terms inconsistently with the source.

actions.label: Document location terminology
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the observed area and location type using the source terms lane, station, and special location rather than assuming the system itself distinguishes the operational meaning.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The map overview and visual location types used as the basis for lane, station, and special location terminology.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed map element cannot be matched to the source-described lane or station visual types.
Escalate if the operational meaning of a special location is required but cannot be confirmed from local site usage.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The presenter says that in RMS world, a station is a station.
A special location could be a sort location and can also be a tipper location; the system does not know the difference by itself.
There are two variants of location: a lane, shown as light blue grayish square blocks, and stations, shown as orange ones.

success_criteria: The user can use the map overview to orient themselves to the system.
The user can separate major areas such as sorter side, charger side, and tipper side on the overview.
The user can correctly identify whether displayed map elements are lanes or stations.
The user recognizes that some special locations require local interpretation.
healthy_conditions: The map overview is visible and usable as a system-level reference.
Major areas on the map can be visually separated.
Light blue or grayish square blocks are recognized as lanes.
Orange elements are recognized as stations.
Special locations are interpreted cautiously using local site context.
failure_conditions: The map overview cannot be opened or viewed.
Displayed map elements cannot be matched to the source-described lane or station visual types.
A special location is assumed to have an operational meaning from RMS alone.
The user cannot distinguish the major system areas referenced in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed map element cannot be matched to the source-described lane or station visual types.
Escalate if the operational meaning of a special location is required but cannot be confirmed from local site usage.
Do not assume the operational meaning of a special location from RMS alone when the source says the distinction depends on local usage.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map overview showing the overall system layout, major areas, and the visual difference between lanes and stations.

missing_details: The source segment does not provide a time estimate for completing this reference procedure.
The source segment does not define supporting roles beyond the operator-oriented viewing task.
The source segment does not provide explicit navigation clicks, menu names, or field labels for reaching the map overview.
The source segment does not define a site-specific method for resolving the operational meaning of a special location.
